name: api-documenter
description: Master API documentation with OpenAPI 3.1, AI-powered tools, and
qualityReason: Strong but overlaps with other doc skills
capabilities: Generate endpoint documentation from code or OpenAPI specs
Create request and response schema definitions
Produce usage code examples in multiple languages
Format documentation with proper markdown structure
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill that generates API documentation. Static analysis found no executable code, no network requests, no filesystem access, and no external command execution. The skill contains only natural language prompts for generating documentation content.
valueStatement: Developers spend hours manually writing API documentation. This skill automates the creation of comprehensive API docs from code or OpenAPI specifications, saving time and ensuring consistency.
limitations: Cannot execute code to test endpoints
Output quality depends on input specification completeness

useCases.title: Quick API Docs for Internal Services
useCases.description: Rapidly generate documentation for internal microservices during development sprints

useCases.targetUser: API product managers and technical writers
useCases.title: Public API Documentation
useCases.description: Create polished, user-facing documentation for public API products

useCases.title: Legacy API Documentation Recovery
useCases.description: Generate documentation for undocumented legacy APIs by analyzing code

promptTemplates.title: Basic Documentation Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate API documentation for the following endpoint: [paste endpoint code or description]
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick documentation for a single endpoint

promptTemplates.title: Full API Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for this OpenAPI specification: [paste OpenAPI JSON/YAML]
promptTemplates.scenario: Complete documentation for an entire API

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Language Examples
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate API documentation with code examples in Python, JavaScript, and curl for: [paste endpoint details]
promptTemplates.scenario: Documentation needed for multiple programming languages

promptTemplates.title: Markdown Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create polished markdown API documentation with sections for overview, endpoints, authentication, error codes, and examples: [paste API specification]

bestPractices: Provide complete OpenAPI specs for best results
Include authentication details in the input
Review generated examples for accuracy before publishing
antiPatterns: Expecting live testing of API endpoints
Providing incomplete or ambiguous endpoint descriptions
Assuming the skill knows your specific internal conventions

faq.question: What input formats does this skill accept?
faq.answer: This skill accepts OpenAPI specifications in JSON or YAML format, as well as natural language descriptions of API endpoints.

faq.question: Can this skill test my API endpoints?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates documentation only. It cannot make actual HTTP requests to test endpoints.

faq.question: What programming languages are supported for code examples?
faq.answer: The skill can generate examples in JavaScript, Python, curl, Ruby, Go, and other common languages.

faq.question: Does this work with GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Currently this skill focuses on REST APIs. GraphQL support may be added in future versions.

faq.question: How accurate is the generated documentation?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on the completeness of your input specification. Full OpenAPI specs produce the most accurate documentation.

faq.question: Can I customize the output format?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify markdown, HTML, or other formats in your prompt. The default output is markdown.
